message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to multiple options for chunked processing; need to edit core.py (Option class, process_value/type_cast_value, info_dict) and types.py. Please propose your files so we can find a disjoint split, possibly splitting core.py by function/section if needed.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for click options (dict-based default={'--opt': {...}}), touching Option class in core.py and possibly types.py for validation. Please share which files/feature you own so we can keep edits disjoint.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option class only)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Parameter base class + Context, everything outside Option class), src/click/types.py
note: My batch_size feature only needs changes inside the Option class in core.py: Option.__init__ (new batch_size param + validation, raise ValueError if negative, TypeError if used without multiple=True), Option.to_info_dict (add batch_size key), and a new Option.type_cast_value override (doesn't touch Parameter.type_cast_value). I will NOT touch Parameter class methods (get_default, process_value, type_cast_value on Parameter, lookup_default etc.) or types.py at all, so your dynamic default resolution work is free to modify those plus types.py without collision. Let me know if this scoping works for you.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/core.py
note: Both our features need core.py's Option class, so let's split it by region instead of by file to avoid clobbering. I only need: (1) tail of Option.__init__ (add ~10 lines validating dict-based dynamic defaults, after your batch_size init code - I'll add mine after whatever you add), (2) a new small Option.get_default override (Option class currently has no get_default override - net-new method), (3) a small elif branch inside existing Option.get_help_extra (~line 2870, next to the existing 'inspect.isfunction... (dynamic)' check), (4) Command.main() - add a second-pass resolution call before ctx.invoke(). I will NOT touch process_value, type_cast_value, or to_info_dict - those are yours. I don't need types.py at all (verified convert_type already falls back to STRING gracefully for non-standard default types via the guessed_type flag, so dict defaults don't break type inference). Go ahead and own types.py fully plus process_value/type_cast_value/to_info_dict/batch_size storage in __init__. Ping me before you touch get_help_extra or add a get_default override so we don't collide.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size validation, Option.to_info_dict, Option.process_value/type_cast_value override), src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ dynamic-default tail, Option.get_default override, Option.get_help_extra elif branch, Command.main second-pass resolution)
note: Agreed. I will add batch_size param + validation as its own block in Option.__init__ (won't touch your dynamic-default validation block), add batch_size to to_info_dict, and add a process_value/type_cast_value override in Option class for chunked batching. I will not touch get_default, get_help_extra, or Command.main. Will ping before touching anything outside this scope. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Parameter base class, Command.main, plus 3 small Option-class insertions: end of Option.__init__ validation block placed immediately before 'def to_info_dict', a brand-new Option.get_default method placed right after that validation block, and one new elif branch inside the existing Option.get_help_extra method), src/click/types.py (only if needed, currently expect no changes)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size param/signature/validation, Option.to_info_dict body, new Option.type_cast_value override)
note: Mostly agree with your split. One correction: I do need 3 small, precisely-anchored additions inside the Option class itself (not just outside it): (1) a short validation block appended at the very end of __init__, right before the 'def to_info_dict' line - I will NOT touch your batch_size param/signature or your validation, just append after whatever you add; (2) a brand-new method Option.get_default (Option has no such override today) inserted right after my validation block, before to_info_dict; (3) one new elif branch inside the existing Option.get_help_extra method (~line 2870, next to 'elif inspect.isfunction(default_value):') to show '(dynamic)' for dict defaults - unrelated to to_info_dict/type_cast_value. I will not touch to_info_dict, type_cast_value, or your batch_size signature/params. Confirm and I'll proceed; please do your __init__/to_info_dict/type_cast_value edits and let me know when done so I anchor after your changes land.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ dynamic-default tail, Option.get_default override, Option.get_help_extra elif branch, Command.main second-pass resolution)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size validation, Option.to_info_dict, Option.process_value/type_cast_value override), src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed, proceeding with implementation now. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size param/validation, Option.to_info_dict, Option.type_cast_value override)
note: Finished my batch_size edits: added batch_size param to Option.__init__ (placed right after 'multiple' in the signature) with validation appended inside the existing final if __debug__ block (raises TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if negative), added batch_size=self.batch_size to Option.to_info_dict, and added a new Option.type_cast_value override that chunks the converted tuple into tuples of up to batch_size items. I did not touch Parameter class, get_default, get_help_extra, or Command.main. Did not need types.py at all - no changes there. Safe for you to anchor your __init__ tail after my validation block (right before 'def to_info_dict') and add get_default/get_help_extra as planned.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ dynamic-default validation, Option.get_default override, Option.get_help_extra elif branch, Command.main second-pass resolution via new module-level helper _resolve_dynamic_option_defaults)
note: Finished dynamic default resolution. All edits landed in my copy of core.py only - no overlap with your batch_size code observed in my working tree, so we appear to be in isolated workspaces despite the shared-file framing. Full test suite (739 tests) passes. Writing patch.txt now.
